title: Bulk report - Week 27
summary:
Capesize
Not a great week for capes with all indices losing value and a larger fall on the T/C routes. The main ore routes of West Australia and Tubarao to China have come off around 70 cents, with the former now in the high 10's and the latter trading around $4.40 level. Both Rio Tinto and BHP are said to have fixed tonnage at this rate.

children.title: Capesize
children.summary: Not a great week for capes with all indices losing value and a larger fall on the T/C routes. The main ore routes of West Australia and Tubarao to China have come off around 70 cents, with the former now in the high 10's and the latter trading around $4.40 level.

children.title: Panamax
children.summary: The initial slide on the panamax index at the start of the week was slowly drawn back, helped by the large volume of fixing during the week from the Asian market. However, the Atlantic basin remained steady with enquiry from the America's whilst the north Atlantic remained less active albeit tonnage here was in limited supply.

children.title: Supramax
children.summary: The week started slow and the US Gulf market has been softer but it showing some resistance now. Grain cargoes from the Black Sea to the Middle East and Pacific lend support in the Atlantic whilst in Asia, nickel ore from Indonesia came back to the market and was moving noticeably again.

children.title: Handysize
children.summary: The first week of July was very uninspired with the Handy index gaining just two points up until time of printing. There was a clear difference between the Atlantic and Pacific basins with the routes from the Continent and east coast South America making steady gains whilst all the remaining routes lost ground.
